website_description: An Information Management Tool for the AI Era
something_wrong: Something went wrong, please try again later
markdown_edit_view_hint: Open edit mode to revise the body directly.
markdown_edit_editing_hint: Saving will mark related AI results as stale.
markdown_edit_stale_hint: After saving, related embeddings, summaries, graphs, podcasts, and presentation results will be marked stale and may need regeneration.
markdown_edit_visual_hint: Edit like rich text while still saving Markdown.
markdown_edit_source_hint: Edit the Markdown source directly for precise formatting and code block fixes.
error_audio_result_not_found: No audio recording available to upload
upload_failed: Upload failed, please try again later
init_setting_alert: Your initialization setup is still incomplete. It is better to finish these defaults soon; until then, uploads, parsing, and AI features will keep reminding you that some default choices are missing.
init_setting_panel_description: Set your default storage, core models, and document parsing engines once so uploads, parsing, summaries, and chat all start from sensible defaults.
init_setting_remaining_summary: {count} required choices are still missing before everything is ready.
init_setting_complete_summary: Your core defaults are ready. You can start using the product now.
init_setting_sidebar_tip_title: These defaults are easy to change later
init_setting_sidebar_tip_description: This flow only helps you pick a good starting configuration. Nothing here is locked in, and every choice can still be changed in Settings.
init_setting_workspace_title: Finish the required defaults in one pass
init_setting_workspace_description: If your account does not have a model or parsing engine yet, add the prerequisite resource first. Once these five defaults are set, the main product flow becomes much smoother.
init_setting_complete_description: Your default file system, models, and parsing engines are all ready. You can start using Revornix now.
init_setting_can_change_later: You can change these later in Settings
init_setting_support_section_description: These cards only matter when the account does not already have a selectable model or parsing engine. If choices already exist, you can skip straight to the required defaults.
init_setting_support_expand_hint: Expand this item when you are ready to configure it
init_setting_model_support_title: Add at least one model first
init_setting_model_support_description: If there is no model to choose from yet, add a provider or model first so you can configure the default chat and summary models.
init_setting_engine_support_title: Enable at least one parsing engine first
init_setting_engine_support_description: If there is no parsing engine available yet, finish that setup first so you can choose the default website and file parsing behavior.
init_setting_required_section_descriptio: These five choices directly affect the default behavior of upload, parsing, summarization, and AI conversations.
init_setting_optional_section_descriptio: These choices do not affect setup completion, but they are worth configuring early if you plan to use the related features.
init_setting_optional_helper: This is optional and does not block setup completion. You can always come back to configure it later.
init_setting_podcast_engine_description: If you plan to generate podcasts from documents or sections, setting a default podcast engine here makes that workflow ready in advance.
init_setting_podcast_engine_missing_hint: There is no podcast engine available yet. You can skip this for now and configure it later after adding a TTS or podcast engine.
init_setting_image_generate_engine_descr: If you plan to generate illustrations or covers inside your content workflow, setting the default image generation engine here saves an extra setup step later.
init_setting_image_generate_engine_missi: There is no image generation engine available yet. You can skip this for now and configure it later after adding image generation support.
init_setting_card_helper: Each change is saved immediately, and you can still adjust it later in Settings.
init_setting_footer_pending: {count} required choices are still unfinished. You can keep using the app now and come back later.
init_setting_footer_done: Setup is complete. You can start using the app normally now.
init_setting_default_file_system_descrip: Uploads, generated assets, and document files will default to this storage. Pick the place you want the system to use first.
init_setting_file_system_missing_hint: There is no file system available yet. Please confirm the system has created your default file system and it should appear here automatically.
init_setting_revornix_ai_model_descripti: This is the default model for Revornix chat, questions, and assistant workflows. It defines the main AI interaction experience.
init_setting_document_summary_model_desc: Document summaries, abstracts, and reading-related AI features will prefer this model by default.
init_setting_model_missing_hint: There is no model available yet. Finish the model setup above first.
init_setting_website_convert_engine_desc: When you import web content, this engine will be used by default to extract the main body and structure.
init_setting_file_convert_engine_descrip: When you upload document files, this engine will be used by default for conversion and structural parsing.
init_setting_engine_missing_hint: There is no parsing engine available yet. Finish the engine setup above first.
init_mine_model_model_provider_choose_ye: Choose a model from the existing provider
init_mine_model_model_provider_choose_no: Create a new model provider and model
init_mine_model_form_needed: You must either: 1) fill in all required fields for a new provider, or 2) select an existing provider
month_summary_description: A chart will display your document reading summary over the past month.
unread_documents_card_description: Documents you added but haven't read yet will appear here.
recent_read_documents_card_description: Your recent reading history will appear here.
dashboard_today_summary_not_created: Today's section has not been generated yet
dashboard_today_summary_not_created_desc: The daily summary section has not been created yet. It will appear here automatically after today's documents finish processing.
dashboard_today_summary_description: This card shows the key attributes of today's section and its audio entry.
dashboard_today_summary_audio_unavailabl: This section audio has not been generated yet.
section_day_notice_description: This section only updates automatically on its own date at the configured time. It will not continue updating on the following day.
section_form_process_task_trigger_schedu: For a daily section, this schedule only runs on its own date at the time configured here. It will not repeat on the following day.
dashboard_today_hot_search_description: This part will display the top 01 hot search from various platforms today.
dashboard_today_hot_search_error_network: Network error occurred while fetching hot search data
dashboard_document_label_summary_tips: A pie chart will display the number of documents for each label.
admin_layout_description: This is a dedicated admin route group for platform-level user, document, and section operations.
admin_overview_description: Open the modules you use most often. Users supports browse, view, create, edit, and delete; documents and sections support browse, view, and delete.
admin_overview_users_description: Browse, view, create, edit, and delete platform users.
admin_overview_documents_description: Browse platform documents and handle common actions such as view and delete.
admin_overview_sections_description: Browse all sections and perform admin actions such as view and delete.
admin_overview_security_description: Review anti-scrape counters, recent gateway hits, and near-limit traffic patterns.
admin_security_description: Review recent anti-scrape counters and gateway-side hit events to spot abuse patterns early.
admin_security_summary_description: Aggregated hit counts grouped by event, policy, and rule over recent rolling windows.
admin_security_summary_empty: No anti-scrape counters have been recorded recently.
admin_security_recent_description: Latest blocked, rate-limited, and near-limit events captured by the gateway.
admin_notification_cover_hint: Upload a real image for the notification cover. Changes take effect after saving.
admin_users_description: Review platform users here and handle common operations such as create, edit, status updates, and delete.
admin_users_search_placeholder: Search by nickname, email, or UUID
admin_users_create_description: Create a new platform account and initialize its default file system.
admin_users_edit_description: Update profile fields, role, or enabled status.
admin_users_delete_description: This will also clean up the user's documents and sections. Delete “{name}”?
admin_users_form_required: Please fill in the required fields first
admin_users_password_placeholder: Leave blank to keep the current password
admin_users_avatar_hint: Upload and preview a real image here. Changes take effect after saving.
admin_users_create_confirm_description: You are about to create user {name}. This will be written to the system.
admin_users_update_confirm_description: You are about to update user {name}. The changes will take effect immediately.
admin_notifications_description: Review this user's notification sources, targets, and tasks, then adjust the task behavior directly.
admin_notifications_sources_description: Notification sources currently available to this user.
admin_notifications_sources_empty: This user has no usable notification sources yet.
admin_notifications_targets_description: Notification targets currently available to this user.
admin_notifications_targets_empty: This user has no usable notification targets yet.
admin_notifications_tasks_description: Create, edit, enable, disable, or delete this user's notification tasks.
admin_notifications_tasks_empty: This user has no notification tasks yet.
admin_notifications_task_form_descriptio: Changes here will apply directly to this user's notification workflow.
admin_notifications_task_form_required: Please fill in the task title, notification source, and notification target first.
admin_notifications_task_custom_required: Custom notifications require both a title and content.
admin_notifications_task_template_requir: Template notifications require a template selection.
admin_notifications_task_event_required: Event-triggered tasks require a trigger event.
admin_notifications_task_scheduler_requi: Scheduled tasks require a Cron expression.
admin_notifications_task_delete_descript: Deleting this task will stop it permanently and cannot be undone.
admin_notifications_task_toggle_descript: You are about to set task {name} to "{status}". The change will take effect immediately.
admin_users_empty_description: Try another keyword or create a new user directly.
admin_documents_description: Browse platform documents, inspect their details, and delete them when necessary.
admin_documents_search_placeholder: Search documents by title or description
admin_documents_view_description: Inspect the document metadata and linked resources.
admin_sections_description: Browse every section, inspect details, and remove sections when needed.
admin_sections_search_placeholder: Search sections by title or description
admin_sections_view_description: Inspect the section metadata and publication state.
notification_receive: You have received a new notification
notification_ios_bind_scan_tip: Scan with your iOS device to bind this target
notification_ios_bind_scan_done_tip: Device bound. No further scan is needed.
notification_feishu_bind_webhook_url_pla: Please enter the Feishu bot webhook URL
notification_feishu_bind_signature_place: Please enter the Feishu bot signature secret (optional)
notification_dingtalk_bind_webhook_url_p: Please enter the DingTalk bot webhook URL
notification_dingtalk_bind_signature_pla: Please enter the DingTalk bot signature secret (optional)
notification_telegram_bind_chat_id_place: Please enter the Telegram chat ID
notification_email_source_host_placehold: Please enter the SMTP host (e.g. smtp.example.com)
notification_ios_source_team_id_placehol: Please enter the Apple Team ID
notification_ios_source_key_id_placehold: Please enter the APNs Key ID
notification_ios_source_private_key_plac: Please enter the APNs private key
notification_feishu_source_app_id_placeh: Please enter the Feishu app ID
notification_feishu_source_app_secret_pl: Please enter the Feishu app secret
notification_telegram_source_bot_token_p: Please enter the Telegram bot token
graph_empty: No related node and relationship data
graph_data: Now there are {node_count} nodes and {edge_count} edges
setting_notification_task_manage_delete_: Are you sure you want to delete? This action cannot be undone after the notification is deleted.
setting_notification_task_manage_form_ta: Please input the title of the notification task
setting_notification_task_manage_form_tr: Here you need to fill in the time Cron expression of the class Unix system, for details please see
setting_notification_task_manage_form_te: Please select the notification content template
setting_notification_task_manage_form_en: Enabled by default. Please disable manually if needed.
setting_notification_task_manage_add_ale: Notification tasks only support event triggers. After you choose an event, the system will automatically match the corresponding template and send notifications when the event happens.
setting_notification_task_manage_update_: Notification tasks only support event triggers. After you update the event, the system will automatically switch to the corresponding template and send notifications when the event happens.
setting_notification_source_manage_delet: Are you sure you want to delete? Once deleted, all related notifications will not be sent.
setting_notification_source_manage_alert: This page not only displays the notification sources you have created yourself, but also shows all notification sources in the Revornix community that users have set to public. You can conveniently use these public notification sources by clicking the Clone button. At the same time, you can also create your own public notification sources for other community members to use. Note: For security reasons, once a notification source is made public, other users can use it but cannot view your engine configuration.
setting_notification_source_manage_form_: Adding a source allows notifications to be sent through various channels.
Public notification sources can be used by all users (but others cannot view your configuration information), private notification sources can only be used by the creator
setting_notification_source_manage_forbi: You do not have permission to modify this notification source
setting_notification_target_manage_delet: Are you sure? After deletion, related notifications can no longer be sent.
setting_notification_target_manage_alert: This page shows both your own targets and public targets from the community. You can use public targets via Fork, or publish your own for others. For security, others can use your public target but cannot view its configuration.
setting_notification_target_manage_empty: No matching notification targets, go to add
setting_notification_target_manage_form_: After adding a target, you can receive notifications through multiple channels.
Public targets are available to all users (configuration stays private). Private targets are only available to the creator.
setting_notification_target_manage_forbi: You do not have permission to modify this notification target
setting_default_document_read_model_upda: Default Document Reading Model Updated Successfully
setting_file_system_page_current_user_fi: Please select the default file system
setting_file_system_confirm_switch_file_: Switching file systems will not automatically migrate files (your original documents and columns/markdown files/static image files/audio files, etc.) from the original file system to the new file system. Please manually migrate files and confirm the switch. Are you sure you want to switch?
setting_file_system_page_alert: Data is not shared between different file systems. If you switch file systems, all files will remain in the original system by default. Please migrate files manually if needed. Creating a new file system will not affect any existing systems.
setting_file_system_page_file_system_for: Please select a file system type
Please enter the file system name
Please enter the file system description
Please enter the file system configuration
setting_file_system_page_file_system_add: Here you can add and configure a file system.
setting_file_system_page_mine_file_syste: Configure parameters for the file system {file_system} here

Are you sure you want to delete it? Once deleted, the file system will no longer be usable (data will remain inside the file system and will not be deleted; if you need to delete the data, please go to the corresponding system to delete it manually)
setting_engine_change_forbidden: You are not allowed to change this engine
setting_engine_page_engine_form_max_conc: The maximum number of tasks this engine can execute at the same time on the backend. Extra requests wait in the queue instead of hitting the upstream provider with unlimited parallelism.
setting_engine_page_mine_engine_is_publi: Public engines can be used by all users (but others cannot view your configuration information), private engines can only be used by the creator
setting_engine_field_api_key_tooltip: The credential used to call the upstream engine API. You can usually create it from the provider dashboard or API key page.
setting_engine_field_openai_api_key_tool: The OpenAI key passed into MarkItDown for richer parsing, especially for images and more complex content.
setting_engine_field_base_url_tooltip: The root endpoint used to send requests. Use the provider default unless you are routing through a proxy, gateway, or self-hosted compatible service.
setting_engine_field_model_name_tooltip: The exact upstream model identifier. This decides which provider model Revornix actually uses.
setting_engine_field_token_tooltip: The access token required by this engine provider. Copy it from the engine service console or developer portal.
setting_engine_field_uid_tooltip: The user identifier issued by the provider. MinerU API uses it in request validation and checksum-related logic.
setting_engine_field_appid_tooltip: The application ID for this engine integration. You can find it in the provider app or project settings page.
setting_engine_field_access_token_toolti: The access token used for authenticated requests to the provider service.
setting_engine_field_access_key_id_toolt: The public key identifier used in signed requests. Create or view it from the cloud provider credential management page.
setting_engine_field_secret_access_key_t: The private secret paired with the access key ID. Most providers only show it once when the key is created.
setting_engine_field_req_key_tooltip: The upstream request key or capability key used by the image service to decide which generation pipeline to run.
setting_engine_field_region_tooltip: The provider region where requests are routed, such as `cn-north-1`.
setting_engine_field_service_tooltip: The provider service code used in signature generation or request routing.
setting_engine_field_action_tooltip: The provider action name used by the API endpoint.
setting_engine_field_version_tooltip: The API version string required by the provider.
setting_engine_field_size_tooltip: The target image size. Follow the provider format, such as `1024x1024` or `2048*2048`.
setting_engine_field_negative_prompt_too: Optional instructions describing what the generated result should avoid.
setting_engine_field_seed_tooltip: Optional numeric seed for more reproducible generation output.
setting_engine_field_scale_tooltip: Optional generation guidance scale used by some image APIs.
setting_engine_field_width_tooltip: Optional explicit output width. Use it when your provider supports width and height separately.
setting_engine_field_height_tooltip: Optional explicit output height. Use it when your provider supports width and height separately.
setting_engine_field_return_url_tooltip: If enabled, the engine prefers returning a hosted image URL when the provider supports it.
setting_engine_field_use_pre_llm_tooltip: If enabled, the upstream flow may run an extra prompt-refinement step before image generation.
setting_engine_field_prompt_extend_toolt: Allows the provider to expand or enhance the prompt automatically before generating the image.
setting_engine_field_watermark_tooltip: Controls whether the provider should add its watermark or watermark metadata to the generated result.
setting_engine_field_generation_mode_too: Controls how the podcast content is generated. `summary` sends long text directly, `prompt` uses topic-based online generation, and `dialogue` generates two-speaker turns first.
setting_engine_field_dialogue_model_id_t: Used by dialogue podcast mode to choose which text model writes the conversation script. If omitted, Revornix falls back to the default document reader model.
setting_engine_field_script_model_id_too: Only used in `dialogue` mode to choose an actually available script-generation model. If omitted, Revornix falls back to the default document reader model.
setting_engine_field_scene_tooltip: An optional provider-specific scene hint that influences upstream generation behavior.
setting_engine_field_speaker_info_toolti: Choose the podcast speaker voices. You can select up to two speakers.
setting_engine_field_speaker_additions_t: Optional JSON map for passing advanced TTS or ICL cloned-voice additions to specific speakers, such as model selection.
setting_engine_field_audio_config_toolti: Advanced output audio settings in JSON, such as format, sample rate, and speech rate.
setting_engine_field_input_info_tooltip: Optional input controls in JSON, including URL mode, truncation limits, per-round character limits, and final audio URL return.
setting_engine_field_aigc_metadata_toolt: Optional metadata payload passed through to the upstream API for implicit watermark headers. It should usually include `enable: true` and only works with supported audio formats such as mp3, wav, and ogg_opus.
setting_engine_field_use_head_music_tool: Whether the generated podcast should add intro music automatically.
setting_engine_field_use_tail_music_tool: Whether the generated podcast should add outro music automatically.
setting_engine_field_aigc_watermark_tool: Whether to include an AIGC watermark marker in the generated audio result.
setting_engine_field_extra_body_tooltip: Extra raw JSON merged directly into the upstream request body. Use this when the provider supports parameters that Revornix has not exposed as dedicated fields yet.
setting_engine_config_invalid_descriptio: The current engine config could not be parsed into a structured form. It will stay untouched unless you edit these fields again.
setting_engine_config_empty_description: This engine does not expose additional connection parameters right now.
setting_engine_config_desc_generation_mo: Use `summary` to summarize long text directly, `prompt` for topic-based online generation, or `dialogue` to generate two-speaker podcast turns first.
setting_engine_config_desc_size: Follow the provider-specific size format, such as `2048*2048` for Bailian or `1024x1024` for compatible services.
setting_engine_config_desc_json: Use valid JSON for this advanced field.
setting_engine_config_desc_prompt_extend: Enable this if you want the provider to automatically enrich your prompt before generation.
setting_engine_config_desc_return_url: Enable this if you prefer the provider to return an image URL instead of inline binary content when supported.
setting_engine_page_engine_add_descripti: Here you can add an engine and configure its parameters.
setting_engine_page_mine_engine_config_d: Configure the parameters of the engine {engine} here
setting_engine_page_mine_engine_delete_a: Are you sure to delete it? Once deleted, the engine will no longer be usable.
setting_default_engine_empty_description: There is no engine available for this account yet. Open engine settings to fork one from the community or create your own, then come back to set the default engine.
setting_default_file_system_empty_descri: There is no file system available yet. Finish the file system setup or add one first.
setting_file_system_field_user_access_ke: Enter your user access key secret
setting_file_system_field_bucket_tooltip: Your storage bucket or container name. You can usually find this in your object storage console where the files are stored.
setting_file_system_field_region_tooltip: The region where this bucket lives, such as `us-east-1` or `oss-cn-beijing`. You can find it on the bucket detail page.
setting_file_system_field_region_id_tool: The OSS region ID from the bucket detail page, such as `oss-cn-beijing`. Use the region ID expected by Aliyun OSS.
setting_file_system_field_region_name_to: The AWS or S3-compatible region name for this bucket, such as `us-east-1`.
setting_file_system_field_endpoint_toolt: The API endpoint used by your storage provider. For S3-compatible services this is often a hostname like `s3.amazonaws.com` or a custom provider endpoint.
setting_file_system_field_endpoint_url_t: The full endpoint URL for uploads, usually copied from your provider documentation or bucket settings. This field should include the `https://` prefix.
setting_file_system_field_public_endpoin: Optional public access endpoint used to read uploaded files back. Use this when your read domain differs from the API upload endpoint.
setting_file_system_field_public_url_too: Optional public base URL used to access files after upload.
setting_file_system_field_custom_domain_: Optional custom CDN or public domain bound to this bucket, if you want generated file URLs to use that domain.
setting_file_system_field_base_path_tool: An optional folder prefix inside the bucket. Uploaded files will be placed under this path instead of the bucket root.
setting_file_system_field_root_path_tool: An optional root folder inside the storage space. Use it to isolate Revornix files from other app data.
setting_file_system_field_prefix_tooltip: An optional path prefix added before uploaded file paths.
setting_file_system_field_access_key_id_: The public identifier for your storage access key pair. Create or view it from your provider IAM, RAM, or access key settings.
setting_file_system_field_access_key_too: The public key or access key ID used to identify your storage credentials.
setting_file_system_field_user_access_ke: The user AccessKey ID used to assume the STS role or access the bucket. Create it from your cloud account credential page.
The user AccessKey Secret paired with the user AccessKey ID. Revornix uses it to obtain temporary credentials or access the bucket.
setting_file_system_field_secret_access_: The private secret paired with your access key ID. This is shown only when the key is created in most providers, so copy it carefully.
setting_file_system_field_access_key_sec: The private secret paired with your access key ID. You can typically generate it from your provider access key page.
setting_file_system_field_secret_key_too: The private credential used with your access key. Treat it like a password and keep it secure.
setting_file_system_field_session_token_: Optional temporary security token used with short-lived credentials such as STS or assumed roles.
setting_file_system_field_force_path_sty: Turn this on for providers that require path-style bucket URLs instead of virtual-host style URLs.
setting_file_system_field_role_arn_toolt: The ARN of the role that Revornix should assume for STS-based access. Copy it from the role detail page in Aliyun RAM or AWS IAM.
setting_file_system_config_builtin_descr: This file system uses the built-in storage flow and does not require extra connection settings.
setting_file_system_config_invalid_descr: The existing file system config could not be parsed into a structured form. It will be preserved until this connection is recreated.
setting_file_system_config_desc_endpoint: Use the full endpoint URL shown by your storage provider, including the protocol.
setting_file_system_config_desc_role_arn: This role should already have the object read/write permissions required by Revornix.
setting_subscription_required_hint: Hosted official features require an eligible subscription tier. If the feature is already unlocked but the monthly allowance is not enough, you can also top up with a compute pack.
setting_subscription_required_hint_compu: Some point-billed hosted features can still be used after the subscription expires, as long as there are unexpired compute points remaining.
default_resource_subscription_locked: The current default requires an active subscription. Update it in Settings or renew first.
setting_official_hosted_tips: When enabled, this model or engine participates in the platform subscription and compute-point logic. When disabled, it is treated as a normal custom resource.
setting_engine_billing_mode_0_descriptio: Charge by the number of input and output tokens consumed.
setting_engine_billing_mode_1_descriptio: Charge once for each invocation, which fits fixed-cost APIs.
setting_engine_billing_mode_2_descriptio: Charge by the number of files processed in upload or batch workflows.
setting_engine_billing_mode_3_descriptio: Charge by the number of pages involved in parsing or conversion.
setting_engine_billing_mode_4_descriptio: Charge by the amount of text processed, useful for translation or extraction flows.
setting_engine_billing_mode_5_descriptio: Charge by the duration of audio or video processing.
setting_engine_billing_mode_6_descriptio: Charge by the number of images generated or processed.
setting_engine_billing_unit_price_placeh: Enter the base points for one unit
setting_engine_billing_unit_price_hint: Base compute points charged for each {unit}.
setting_engine_billing_preview_formula: Final charge = usage count x {unit_price} points per {unit} x multiplier {multiplier}
setting_compute_point_multiplier_tips: Use this for higher-cost scenarios. Values above 1 further scale the base charge.
setting_required_plan_level_tips: Choose which subscription tiers can use this model or engine.
setting_subscription_locked_reason_upgra: Upgrade your current plan to access this
setting_subscription_locked_reason_renew: Renew your current plan to continue access
setting_default_model_empty_description: There is no model available for this account yet. Open model settings to fork one from the community or create your own, then come back to set the default model.
ai_model_provider_community_tips_descrip: This page not only displays the model providers you have created yourself, but also shows all model providers in the Revornix community that users have set to public. You can conveniently use these public model providers by clicking the Fork button. After forking, you can configure the models provided by these forked model providers on your settings page.At the same time, you can also create your own public model providers for other community members to use. Note: For security reasons, after a model provider is made public, other users can use your models but cannot see your model API keys or URLs.
engine_community_tips_description: This page not only displays the engines you have created yourself, but also shows all engines in the Revornix community that users have set to public. You can conveniently use these public engines by clicking the Clone button. At the same time,you can also create your own public engines for other community members to use. Note: For security reasons, once an engine is made public, other users can use your engine but cannot view its configuration.
setting_model_provider_name_placeholder: Please input the model provider name
setting_model_provider_description_place: Please input the model provider description
setting_model_provider_is_public_tips: Public model providers can be used by all users (but others cannot view your model key and URL information), private model providers can only be used by the creator
setting_model_provider_add_description: You can quickly add a model provider here, or use your own custom service, but note that the service must comply with the OpenAI protocol.
setting_model_provider_delete_warning_de: Once a model provider is deleted, all related model groups will also be deleted. This action is irreversible! Are you sure you want to delete?
setting_model_models_configure_descripti: Here you can configure all models available from the corresponding provider
setting_model_collection_manage_hint: Manage the models under this provider, including plan access and official hosted billing behavior.
setting_model_add_failed: Failed to create model, please try again later
setting_model_update_failed: Failed to update model, please try again later
setting_model_delete_warning_description: Deleting this model removes it from the current provider and cannot be undone. Do you want to continue?
setting_model_card_add_description: Create a new model and configure its plan access plus official hosted billing behavior.
setting_model_card_edit_description: Update the model name, plan access, and official hosted billing settings.
setting_model_card_access_ok: Users matching the selected subscription level can use this model
setting_model_card_billing_hosted: This model participates in official hosted billing and uses the current multiplier for compute charges.
setting_model_card_billing_custom: This model is treated as a normal custom resource and does not participate in official hosted billing.
setting_revornix_model_empty: The default model is not configured yet

account_plan_tips: Revornix plans mainly cover platform features, collaboration, and monthly hosted AI allowance. Buying the same plan extends its validity, some higher-cost hosted features consume points at higher multipliers, and direct downgrades are not currently supported.
account_plan_subscription_notes_checkout: Apple auto-renew subscriptions and website purchases can coexist. If you already have Apple billing, a higher website plan takes effect first and falls back to the Apple tier after the coverage ends.
account_plan_subscription_notes_points: When upgrading from Pro to Max, the current cycle gets a prorated point top-up based on the remaining time. The next full cycle then receives the full Max monthly allowance. Newly granted points remain valid for 3 months.
account_plan_subscription_checkout_notic: If you already have an Apple subscription, a higher website plan takes effect first and falls back to the Apple tier after the coverage ends.
account_plan_pay_qr_code_description: Please scan to make the payment. The current QR code will remain valid for
account_plan_pay_warning: Please confirm the transaction details before proceeding with payment.
account_plan_pay_way_choose_description: Choose a payment method. Currently, only Alipay / WeChat Pay / PayPal are supported. Alipay / WeChat Pay only support RMB payment, PayPal only supports USD payment.
account_plan_upgrade_points_notice: If you are on Pro and upgrade to Max, the current cycle gets a prorated top-up for the point difference based on the remaining time. The next full cycle then receives the full Max monthly allowance.
account_plan_custom_plan_description: For teams, private deployment, dedicated allowance, and more complex commercial needs. Custom plans currently start above the Max tier, so please contact support for details.
account_plan_compute_pack_description: Hosted compute packs cover official AI usage for Revornix AI, official image generation, official podcast generation, and similar hosted features. Points are deducted from the batch that expires soonest, and some higher-cost models or engines consume points at higher multipliers.
account_plan_compute_pack_multiplier_hin: Some official hosted models or engines can consume points at higher multipliers. The exact multiplier is determined by the model or engine configuration.

auth_shell_title: Bring your documents, sections, and AI workflows into one knowledge surface.
auth_shell_description: Revornix combines content capture, organization, graphing, and AI-assisted retrieval in one workspace so knowledge can be searched, connected, and reused.
auth_shell_feature_capture_title: Capture every input in one place
auth_shell_feature_capture_description: Pull web pages, documents, and section materials into the same workspace so nothing gets scattered.
auth_shell_feature_organize_description: Use labels, graphs, and section relationships to organize content for easier retrieval and review.
auth_shell_feature_assist_title: Make AI work with your context
auth_shell_feature_assist_description: Ask, summarize, and connect ideas against your own knowledge base so responses stay grounded in real material.
auth_legal_notice_prefix: By logging in or registering, you agree to our
seo_home_description: Discover public sections, creator profiles, and linked documents published on Revornix. Start from the community feed and continue into topic-focused knowledge collections.
seo_home_heading: Explore public sections before you sign in
seo_home_intro: Revornix is an AI-native workspace for collecting documents, connecting context, and publishing knowledge collections as public sections. Browse the latest public feed, open creator profiles, and continue into linked documents to understand each topic in depth.
seo_home_body_1: Unlike a closed dashboard, the public area is designed for discovery. Community sections surface research themes, workflow notes, curated references, and AI-generated structure that can be read without an account.
seo_home_body_2: If you want the broadest entry point, start with the community page. From there, each section can lead you to its creator profile, related documents, markdown content, graph view, and discussion.
seo_home_learn_1: Community pages list the latest published sections and topic matches.
seo_home_learn_2: Section pages expose summaries, markdown content, related documents, and creator context.
seo_home_learn_3: Creator pages group public work so search visitors can continue exploring connected topics.
seo_community_description: Start from the public section feed, then continue into creator pages and linked documents. This mirrors how anonymous visitors naturally browse promoted content.
seo_community_documents_description: Browse publicly published documents directly to reach source material, transcripts, markdown text, and supporting context without first going through a section overview.
seo_community_search_placeholder: Search public section titles or descriptions
seo_community_documents_search_placehold: Search public document titles or descriptions
seo_community_intro_title: How to browse the public knowledge graph
seo_community_intro_paragraph_1: Revornix Community is the public discovery layer for shared sections. Each section collects related documents, AI-generated structure, creator context, and ongoing updates so visitors can understand a topic from one entry point instead of jumping between isolated files.
seo_community_intro_paragraph_2: If you are arriving from search, start with a section summary, open the connected creator profile, and then continue into the linked documents. That path usually reveals the most useful context around a workflow, research theme, or curated knowledge collection.
seo_community_intro_paragraph_3: This page is updated as creators publish new sections, expand document sets, and refine descriptions. Browse the latest entries below or search by topic to find public sections that match your area of interest.
seo_community_documents_intro_paragraph_: The public document view is the fastest way to start from a single published asset. It lets visitors reach source material immediately instead of always beginning with a section summary.

When a document already has markdown text, transcripts, or generated supporting content, this view can serve search visitors more directly and then lead them deeper into related sections or creator pages.
Everything shown here has already been published publicly. Search by topic to find public files, websites, quick notes, and audio-based documents.
seo_community_explore_title: What you can explore from here
seo_community_explore_sections: Open a public section to read its overview, markdown content, discussion, graph view, and related documents.
seo_community_explore_creators: Visit creator profile pages to review the rest of a publisher's public work and topical focus.
seo_community_explore_documents: Follow linked documents for source material, readable text, transcripts, and supporting references.
seo_community_documents_explore_document: Open public documents directly to read their body text, summaries, transcripts, and supporting details.
seo_community_documents_explore_sources: Start with documents that already expose markdown or transcript content for the clearest reading experience.
seo_community_documents_explore_sections: If a document belongs to a published section, continue there for broader context and related materials.
seo_community_empty: No public sections matched your search
seo_community_empty_description: Try another keyword or return to the latest public sections.
seo_community_documents_empty: No public documents matched your search
seo_community_documents_empty_descriptio: Try another keyword or return to the latest published documents.
seo_user_intro_with_slogan: {name} publishes public sections on Revornix and uses this profile as the main public hub for ongoing work, curated topics, and related knowledge collections.
seo_user_intro_without_slogan: {name} uses this public profile to group published sections, creator context, and linked knowledge collections that visitors can continue browsing on Revornix.
seo_user_explore_title: How to continue from this page
seo_user_explore_sections: Browse the creator's public sections below to follow topic-specific work and recent updates.
seo_user_explore_documents: Switch to the documents tab to open the creator's published source material, notes, files, and audio documents directly.
seo_user_explore_community: Return to the community feed to discover more creators and related public sections.
seo_document_meta_description: Keep the document metadata together in the right sidebar so the reading flow stays focused on the content.
seo_document_source_quick_note: This document was written manually by the user as a quick note, so there is no separate external source or attached asset.
seo_document_source_default: This document does not have a separate external source or attached asset.
seo_document_related_sections_descriptio: Jumping back from a document to its section is the most natural way for visitors to recover the broader context.
seo_document_related_sections_empty: There are no publicly linked sections for this document.
seo_section_login_to_comment: Sign in to join the discussion for this public section.
seo_document_login_to_comment: Sign in to join the discussion for this public document.
seo_document_notes_description: Supplementary notes appended by readers of this document.
seo_document_notes_empty_description: No reader has appended a note yet.
